Off Calendar Aug-06-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person(S) Most Qualified Of Defendant 3850 18th Street Housing Associates, L.P., A California Limited Partnership; And The Person(S) Most Qualified Of Defendant Bridge Property Management Company, A California Corporation, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Defendants' Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MAHDI BOUKHAROUBA's, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person(S) Most Qualified Of Defendant 3850 18th Street Housing Associates, L.P., A California Limited Partnership; And The Person(S) Most Qualified Of Defendant Bridge Property Management Company, A California Corporation, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Defendants' Counsel.

Plaintiff Mahdi Boukharouba's motion to compel the depositions of the persons most qualified of both defendants and production of documents by the deponents, and for monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety. Because defendants have agreed to produce deponents for both depositions ("one to complete and one to commence"), the portion of the motion seeking to compel the attendance of the deponents is moot. Nowhere In plaintiff's moving papers is there any identification of what documents plaintiff asks this court to compel the deponents to produce, much less a separate statement as to those documents, and thus the portion of the motion seeking production of documents is denied. While plaintiff's counsel asserts that he "attempted to meet and confer," he does not cite any evidence that he did so and thus the portion of the motion seeking sanctions is denied for failure to meet and confer.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)
